name: Kickoff
description: Runs a planning-only session that produces CLAUDE.md, a plan file, and a ready-to-paste build prompt for session 2. Asks clarifying questions, explores the codebase, and outputs everything you need to hand off to a build session.
author: Z Huang
example: /kickoff → asks questions about goals and constraints → writes CLAUDE.md + plan.md + build prompt

name: Build
tagline: Execute the plan, no questions asked
description: Use for session 2 after /kickoff. Reads plan.md and CLAUDE.md, then implements all steps in order without asking questions. Makes reasonable decisions and keeps moving until the build is complete.
author: Z Huang
example: /build → reads plan.md → implements step by step → reports when done

name: Resume
tagline: Pick up where you left off
description: Reads CLAUDE.md, git log, git status, git diff, and TODO/FIXME markers to summarize what was being worked on, the current state, and the obvious next step. Perfect for starting a new session on an existing project.
author: Z Huang
triggers: pick up where I left off
example: /resume → reads project state → "You were implementing the search feature. Step 3 of 5 is done. Next: add filter pills."

name: Checkpoint
description: Mid-build save and preview. Runs type checks, sanity checks, git push, starts a local server, and takes a screenshot so you can see progress without breaking flow.
author: Z Huang
example: /checkpoint → type checks pass → git push → serves at localhost:3000 → takes screenshot

name: Ship It
description: Full deploy pipeline — runs a 15-check audit with auto-fix, then commits, pushes, and deploys to Cloudflare Pages. The last step before your project goes live.
author: Z Huang
example: /shipit → 15 checks (entry point, git, meta tags, links...) → auto-fixes 3 issues → deploys to Cloudflare Pages

name: Verify
description: Automated quality gate — checks 14 categories of objective criteria after a build, auto-fixes issues in a loop, and proves the build is clean. Run this after /build to catch problems before deploying.
author: Z Huang
example: /verify → checks responsive layout, console errors, links, accessibility → auto-fixes 2 issues → all 14 categories pass

name: Review
description: Checks your site against 12 categories: broken links, SEO, layout, typography, copy, mobile, performance, visual polish, interactions, security, and code quality. Auto-fixes what it can.
author: Z Huang
example: /review → finds 8 issues (missing meta tags, low contrast text, broken link) → fixes 5 automatically → reports remaining 3

name: Roast
description: Quick tech review — 10 engineering personas (security, performance, UX, code quality, DevOps, API, accessibility, data, architecture, QA) each give a grade and their top findings in a single pass. Auto-fixes objective issues.
author: Z Huang
example: /roast → Security: B+ (1 issue), Performance: A-, UX: B (3 issues)... → auto-fixes 4 objective issues

name: Expert Panel
description: Intensive multi-round expert review — 10 executive personas each do 3 rounds of critique with 5 fixes per round (150 findings total), followed by a 30-fix anti-AI text audit. The most thorough review skill available.
author: Z Huang
example: /expert-panel → Round 1: 50 findings, 30 fixed → Round 2: 50 more → Round 3: 50 more → anti-AI text audit

name: Beta Test
description: Tests a web app end-to-end as a real user — navigates every page with Playwright, fills forms, tests interactions, catches bugs (console errors, broken i18n, dead links, CSP issues), produces a structured report with severity scores, then triages and fixes findings one by one.
author: Z Huang
example: /beta-test → navigates 12 pages → finds 5 bugs (2 critical, 3 minor) → fixes all 5 → re-tests → clean

name: Test Feature
description: Tests if a specific feature or function works as designed — explores the code, builds a test plan, runs tests, diagnoses and fixes failures in a loop, and reports results.
author: Z Huang
example: /test-feature search → explores search code → tests 5 scenarios → 1 fails (empty query) → fixes → all pass

name: Frontend Design
tagline: Production-grade UI that doesn't look AI-generated
description: Builds web components, pages, or apps from scratch with distinctive, production-grade frontend code that avoids generic AI aesthetics. Produces polished results with intentional design choices.
author: Z Huang
example: /frontend-design hero section → proposes 3 design vibes → builds chosen style with Tailwind + custom animations

name: Design Match
tagline: Match your site to a reference design
description: Analyzes a reference design's visual weight (structure, density, colors), extracts measured values, and iterates section-by-section with 1-5 scoring until your site is near-identical to the target.
author: Z Huang
example: /design-match [reference URL] → extracts spacing, colors, typography → iterates 3 rounds → score: 4.5/5

name: UI/UX Pro Max
description: UI/UX design toolkit with 67 styles, 96 palettes, 57 font pairings, 25 chart types, and 13 framework stacks including React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, and SwiftUI. Can plan, build, review, and optimize.
author: Z Huang
example: /ui-ux-pro-max review → audits color, typography, spacing, interactions → suggests 12 improvements with code

name: TDD UI
description: Takes a before screenshot, makes the UI edit, takes an after screenshot, evaluates the result, and gives a pass/fail verdict. Test-driven development for visual changes.
author: Z Huang
example: /tdd-ui change button color → before screenshot → edits CSS → after screenshot → compares → pass

name: Research
description: Researches any topic by searching the web, reading local files, and writing focused markdown reports to a folder. Great for exploring new technologies, comparing options, or investigating issues.
author: Z Huang
example: /research best auth libraries for Next.js → searches web → reads docs → writes comparison report

name: Scrape
description: Scrapes websites, ingests local files/PDFs/YouTube into a corpus, builds a search index, and auto-tags content. Useful for building knowledge bases or collecting structured data.
author: Z Huang
example: /scrape https://example.com → crawls 50 pages → extracts text → builds searchable corpus

name: Translate
description: Translates content between English and Chinese. Supports Bible verses (和合本 CUV), UI strings, and general text. Returns structured output with original and translated text.
author: Z Huang
example: /translate "God is love" → 神就是爱 (1 John 4:8, 和合本)

name: Last 30 Days
tagline: What people are saying right now
description: Researches what people are saying about a topic in the last 30 days — searches Reddit, X/Twitter, and the web for current discussions, recommendations, and debates, then produces a structured briefing.
author: Z Huang
example: /last30days Claude Code → finds 45 discussions → sentiment: 82% positive → top complaints: context limits, pricing

name: Learn
description: Reflects on the conversation and updates memory — scans for learnings across 7 categories, updates auto-memory, CLAUDE.md, and Obsidian vault. Can run anytime mid-session to capture what you've learned.
author: Z Huang
example: /learn → scans conversation → saves 3 feedback memories, updates CLAUDE.md with new convention

name: Showcase
description: Creates instructional videos or demo materials for completed projects — generates scripts, pushes to NotebookLM, and produces steering prompts for Video Overview generation.
author: Z Huang
example: /showcase → reads codebase → generates 3-minute script → pushes to NotebookLM → outputs video steering prompt

name: Brief
tagline: Project briefing PDF in one command
description: Generates a project briefing PDF for sharing with colleagues. Reads the codebase, takes live screenshots, produces a PRD, and converts to PDF — all automatically.
author: Z Huang
example: /brief → reads code + screenshots → writes PRD → converts to PDF → saves to project folder

name: NotebookLM
description: Pushes local markdown files into Google NotebookLM and generates deliverables — briefings, podcasts, mind maps, flashcards, study guides, and FAQs. All free, all automated.
author: Z Huang
example: /notebooklm → selects 5 markdown files → uploads to NotebookLM → generates podcast + mind map

name: YouTube Research
description: Searches YouTube for videos, pushes them into a Google NotebookLM notebook, runs AI analysis, and generates deliverables (infographics, podcasts, flashcards, mind maps, slide decks, study guides) — all in one command.
author: Z Huang
example: /ytr "Claude Code tutorials" → finds 10 videos → uploads to NotebookLM → generates podcast + flashcards

name: Refactor
description: Improves code quality — tackles duplicated code, naming, readability, dead code, efficiency, and general cleanup. Analyzes before changing, explains trade-offs.
author: Z Huang
example: /refactor src/utils.ts → finds 3 duplicated patterns → consolidates into shared helpers → reduces 180 lines to 95

name: Google Forms
description: Creates or edits Google Forms programmatically — generates Apps Script code to build forms. Never uses the Forms UI, always injects code via the Apps Script editor.
author: Z Huang
example: /google-forms feedback survey → generates Apps Script → 10 questions with validation + email collection

name: Playwright CLI
description: Automates browser interactions — navigating to URLs, taking screenshots, filling forms, clicking buttons, extracting data, and generating PDFs. All from the command line.
author: Z Huang
example: /playwright-cli screenshot https://example.com → opens browser → takes full-page screenshot → saves to project

name: Skill Creator
description: Creates new skills, modifies existing ones, runs skill evals, benchmarks performance, and optimizes skill descriptions. The meta-skill for building skills.
author: Z Huang
example: /skill-creator deploy-check → writes skill.md → runs 3 eval scenarios → optimizes trigger description
